us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Supreme Court justices set rare congressional testimony on security funding
bullets: Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett were scheduled to testify before Congress.
The hearing focuses on funding and safety concerns after threats against judges.
It follows a contentious court term involving rulings on regulatory power and tariffs.

articles.title: ICE says Maine officer fatally shot driver while fearing for public safety
bullets: An ICE officer fatally shot a motorist in Biddeford, Maine.
Sen. Angus King said DHS told him the man allegedly tried to use a vehicle as a weapon.
Officials said the agents involved did not have body cameras.

articles.title: Federal evidence turned over in Minnesota killings after months of delay
bullets: Federal officials turned over evidence tied to the Renee Good and Alex Pretti killings.
Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison said the delay lasted more than half a year.
The handoff followed renewed talks among the FBI, U.S. Attorney’s Office and state officials.

articles.title: Trump order scales back Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante monuments
bullets: President Donald Trump announced reductions to two Utah national monuments.
The move revisits actions from his first term that were later reversed.
The sites were protected under the Antiquities Act for cultural, historic and scientific value.

articles.title: Darline Graham Nordone named temporary replacement for late Sen. Lindsey Graham
bullets: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster appointed Graham’s sister to the Senate seat.
Nordone will serve the remaining months of Graham’s term until January.
A special primary next month will determine the Republican nominee for the November election.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: US strikes Iran as Tehran retaliates across the Middle East
bullets: The U.S. launched new strikes on Iran early Tuesday.
Iran responded with attacks on U.S. allies in the Middle East.
The fighting threatened an interim deal meant to pause the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Hungary passes amendment to remove Orbán-era president
bullets: Hungary’s Parliament passed a constitutional amendment to remove President Tamás Sulyok.
Prime Minister Péter Magyar’s Tisza party used its two-thirds majority to advance the measure.
The amendment passed 139–6 while Orbán’s Fidesz party boycotted the session.

articles.title: Yemen’s Houthis strike Saudi Arabia’s Abha airport with missiles and drones
bullets: Houthi rebels said they attacked Abha International Airport on Monday.
They framed the attack as retaliation for strikes on Sanaa International Airport.
AP reported no casualties, but described the exchange as a sharp escalation.

articles.title: Experts say fully securing Strait of Hormuz could require much larger US force
bullets: Experts told AP that reopening the strait may require more U.S. warships or ground forces.
Commercial vessels have avoided some routes because of mines and attack risks.
The strait remains central to global oil and natural gas trade.

articles.title: China’s ‘Green Great Wall’ slows desert growth but challenges remain
bullets: China has used straw checkerboards and vegetation to fight desertification.
The effort aims to reduce sandstorms and stabilize expanding desert areas.
Scientists warned the long-running campaign still faces climate and sustainability limits.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Indian shares open lower as Middle East tensions lift oil prices
bullets: Indian equities opened lower Tuesday as oil prices rose.
Reuters reported renewed Middle East tensions raised inflation worries.
India is a major oil importer, making crude-price moves important for markets.

articles.title: India bans imports of goods made with forced labour
bullets: India prohibited imports of goods produced or manufactured using forced labour.
The commerce ministry issued the notification Tuesday.
The move adds a forced-labour compliance requirement to import rules.

articles.title: India protests to Iran over killing of seafarer in Hormuz
bullets: India summoned Iran’s deputy ambassador after an Indian seafarer was killed.
The foreign ministry lodged a strong protest over the incident in the Strait of Hormuz.
India said attacks on commercial shipping and civilian infrastructure must cease.

articles.title: India says it will combat organized crime after US indictment in Canada murder case
bullets: India said it remains committed to working with partners against transnational organized crime.
The statement followed a U.S. indictment tied to the 2023 killing of a Sikh separatist leader in Canada.
Reuters reported the comments came a week after the U.S. charges were announced.

articles.title: Rupee slides past 96 per dollar as oil climbs and RBI likely intervenes
bullets: The rupee weakened past 96 per U.S. dollar to a one-month low.
Reuters linked the move to higher oil prices and renewed Middle East conflict fears.
Traders said the Reserve Bank of India likely intervened to manage volatility.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top US market movers from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: JPMorgan profit rises, but stock slips after expenses forecast
bullets: JPMorgan reported higher second-quarter profit on strong dealmaking and IPO activity.
Reuters reported the bank also benefited from volatile markets and stock trading.
Shares fell in premarket trading after the bank raised its 2026 expense forecast.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs beats profit estimates on trading surge and deal spree
bullets: Goldman Sachs exceeded second-quarter profit expectations.
Reuters reported market volatility boosted equities revenue to a record.
Large corporate deals supported investment-banking fees.

articles.title: IBM sinks after warning AI spending is squeezing software budgets
bullets: IBM forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates.
Reuters reported customers shifted spending toward AI infrastructure over software.
The warning triggered a broader sell-off in software stocks.

articles.title: PepsiCo draws activist attention as Elliott takes $4 billion stake
bullets: CNBC listed Elliott’s $4 billion PepsiCo stake among the day’s major market-moving items.
The activist investment put PepsiCo in focus during Tuesday trading.
PepsiCo shares were active alongside other consumer-staples names.

articles.title: US stocks waver after CPI report as oil jumps on Middle East fighting
bullets: U.S. stock futures wavered after the June CPI report.
AP reported oil prices rose as fighting flared again in the Middle East.
Investors watched whether company earnings could justify elevated stock valuations.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Top corporate and economic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Twelve states sue to block Paramount takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery
bullets: A coalition of 12 states sued to block Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery.
The lawsuit argues the $81 billion deal would reduce competition in Hollywood.
Paramount said it would vigorously defend the transaction.

articles.title: JPMorgan posts $16.9 billion quarterly profit on trading and market volatility
bullets: JPMorgan reported $16.9 billion in second-quarter profit.
Revenue in every major business line reached records, according to AP.
Markets revenue grew 35% from the same period last year.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs reports $20.98 quarterly earnings per share
bullets: Goldman Sachs reported second-quarter earnings per common share of $20.98.
The firm said annualized return on common equity was 23.5%.
The results were released Tuesday by Goldman Sachs.

articles.title: Economists warn institutions must act on AI job-displacement risks
bullets: Hundreds of economists and technologists signed an open letter on AI’s economic impact.
The statement warned AI could cause large-scale job displacement.
Signatories included figures tied to Anthropic, Google and OpenAI.

articles.title: AI data-center boom adds inflation pressure for consumers and the Fed
bullets: AP reported AI data-center investment could top $700 billion this year.
Demand for chips, processors and electricity is pushing some costs higher.
Economists expect the spending surge to keep inflation pressure elevated.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: EU chief weighs age restrictions for children on social media
bullets: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen cited addictive platform design.
The EU is considering tighter limits on children’s social media use.
The comments followed a report on children’s online safety in Brussels.

articles.title: Renewable advocates push cleaner power for AI data centers
bullets: AI data-center growth is increasing electricity demand.
AP reported new gas plants are rising to serve that demand.
Clean-energy supporters are pressing for renewable alternatives.

articles.title: Meta appeals landmark social media addiction verdict
bullets: Meta appealed a Los Angeles jury verdict in a social media addiction case.
The verdict found Meta responsible for designing platforms that hooked young users.
Meta’s lawyers filed the notice of appeal Tuesday in Los Angeles County Superior Court.

articles.title: AI economic-impact letter draws signatures from economists and tech leaders
bullets: An open letter warned that AI could transform the economy rapidly.
It said institutions should act now on displacement risks and opportunities.
The letter was organized by Stanford University’s digital economy lab.

articles.title: IBM says AI infrastructure spending is crowding out software budgets
bullets: IBM forecast revenue below estimates, citing a shift in customer spending.
Reuters said customers favored servers, storage and memory tied to AI demand.
The warning hit IBM shares and pressured software-sector sentiment.
